back_translation: Therefore, what is the advantage of being a Jew? Or what is the benefit of circumcision?
Much, in every respect. First, they have been entrusted to keep the (royal) word of God
Then what? If some did not believe, will their unbelief render void the faithfulness of God?

Absolutely not — let God be (royally) truthful, and let every human being be a liar; just as it is written: 'In order that You (royally) may demonstrate righteousness in Your (royal) word, and (royally) prevail when You are (royally) judged'
But if our unrighteousness lifts up the righteousness of God, what shall we say? Is God unrighteous, the One who (royally) brings (royal) wrath? I speak in a human manner
Absolutely not — if so, how would God (royally) judge the world?
But if the truth of God overflows through my falsehood, toward His (royal) glory, why am I still being judged as a sinner?
And why not say, as some slander us by saying — and as some maintain that we teach — 'Let us do evil so that good may come'? The judgment of such people is just
So what then? Are we any better? Not at all, for we have already laid the charge that both Jews and Greeks alike are all under sin
Just as it is written: 'There is no righteous one — not even one
There is none who understands; there is none who seeks God
All have turned away; together they have become useless. There is none who does good — not even one'
'Their throats are an open grave; their tongues speak deceitfully' 'The venom of cobras is under their lips'
'Their mouths are full of cursing and bitterness'
'Their feet are swift to shed blood
Destruction and misery are in their paths
And the way of peace they have never known'
'There is no fear of God before their eyes'
Now we know that whatever the Law says, the Law speaks to those who are under the Law — so that every mouth may be fully shut, and the whole world may come under the (royal) judgment of God
Therefore, no flesh will be (royally) declared righteous before His (royal) presence by conducting oneself according to the Law — for through the Law, the knowledge of sin arises
But now — apart from the Law — the righteousness of God has been (royally) revealed, with the Law and the prophets bearing witness
This righteousness of God comes through faith in Jesus Christ, to all who believe — for there is no difference
For all have sinned, and lack the (royal) glory of God
And are (royally) declared righteous freely, by His grace, through the redemption that is in Christ Jesus

God (royally) set Him forth as a sin-atoning sacrifice, by means of faith, in His (royal) blood — to demonstrate the righteousness of God, because in His patience, He (royally) refrained from punishing the sins committed beforehand
In the patience of God — to demonstrate His righteousness in this present time, so that He may (royally) be the righteous One, and (royally) declare as righteous the one who has faith in Jesus
Therefore, where is the boasting? It has been excluded already. By what kind of principle? By the principle of conduct? No — but by the principle of faith
For we reckon that a human being is (royally) declared righteous by faith — apart from conducting oneself according to the Law
Or is God (royally) the God of Jews only? Is He not (royally) also of Gentiles? Yes — of Gentiles too
Since there is one God, the One who (royally) declares as righteous those who are circumcised by faith, and (royally) declares as righteous those who are not circumcised through that same faith
Therefore, do we render void the Law by faith? Absolutely not — but rather we uphold the Law to stand firm
